title: U Michigan - Sirius Red staining
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bk8pb","text":"Summary: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8qck3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dodbh","text":"Picrosirius red staining (also called Sirius red staining) highlights fibrosis by staining collagen (collagen 1 and III fibers) fibers in paraffin-embedded kidney tissue sections. Sirius red stained collagen appears red by light microscopy. When polarized, the birefringence is highly specific for collagen. The larger collagen fibers are bright yellow or orange, and the thinner ones, including reticular fibers, are green.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Wash in running tap water for 3 min
Wash in running tap water for 10 min
Incubate for 60 min in Picrosirius red solution (do not shorten this time)
Wash 2x for 5 min in acidified H ² O (Change after each wash)
Mount slides with mounting medium - 1 drop
Insert cover glass carefully, avoid bubble
creator: Lili Liang
